refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each addressing a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements into one and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a model or map representing the terrain of the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating key site features relevant to planning and safety
Develop a layered worksite model combining terrain and operational feature data
Produce a geospatial representation integrating terrain and policy constraints
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a layered worksite model combining terrain and operational feature data

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and operational data layers may introduce dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By structuring the worksite model in layers (terrain, features, policies), each layer can be updated independently, supporting functional independence and reducing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data
Risk that operational policies may change frequently requiring flexible model updates
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume layered model data formats
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of 'site features' to ensure all necessary data types are included and specify interface requirements for planning/execution functions to consume the worksite model.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the software components responsible for environmental modeling, data fusion, and situational awareness, including the development of layered worksite models that combine terrain and operational feature data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and terrain data inputs necessary for building the worksite model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands that may influence model updates or focus areas.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with other system modules and external data sources for model updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the worksite model is core to environmental awareness handled by NGHE; minimal diffusion as data inputs come through defined interfaces.
Vehicle module uses the model but does not develop it; would require significant diffusion to implement model development here.
Teleoperation consumes model data for remote control but is not responsible for model development; high diffusion if contained here.
Safety Services may use model data for hazard detection but is not responsible for model creation; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of developing a layered worksite model, as it aligns with environmental data processing and situational awareness responsibilities, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may affect model accuracy.
Integration complexity with other modules requiring model data access.
Risk of scope creep if NGHE Solution module becomes too large or complex.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module. If diffusion issues arise, consider refining interfaces or splitting NGHE Solution into submodules for better modularity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of developing a layered worksite model combining terrain and operational feature data directly addresses the FR of providing worksite area analysis including terrain representation. The DP logically decomposes the FR into a concrete design parameter that fulfills the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may affect model accuracy.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has high cohesion (containment score 9) and low diffusion risk (diffusion score 2). The module boundary aligns with environmental modeling and situational awareness responsibilities, minimizing diffusion across other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives defined inputs (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) and produces the layered worksite model accessible to planning/execution functions, enabling traceability from FR to IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module for implementing the DP ensures ownership and architectural clarity, supporting verification and maintenance.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module. If diffusion issues arise, consider refining interfaces or splitting NGHE Solution into submodules for better modularity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, containment, and verification feasibility. The DP is well contained within a high-cohesion, low-diffusion module (NGHE Solution), and the functional requirement is fully addressed by the DP. Although there is a potential risk related to telemetry data dependency, it is acknowledged and manageable within the current architecture. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition ensures clear ownership and traceability, supporting verification and maintenance. Therefore, no further decomposition is necessary at this time.

assumptions: Telemetry data will be timely and accurate enough to support model accuracy.
NGHE Solution module will maintain high cohesion and low diffusion as designed.
Verification processes will be implemented to trace from FR through DP to IM.
open_risks: Dependency on telemetry data accuracy and timeliness may impact model accuracy.
evidence_needed: Verification results demonstrating traceability from FR to IM.
Monitoring data on telemetry data quality and its impact on model accuracy.
